name: White Chocolate Mousse-Chef Apuzzo of Andrea's Restaurant
recipeInstructions:
White chocolate is not actual chocolate but cocoa butter with the chocolate liquor removed. This dessert was originally conceived as a dark chocolate mousse by Chef Andrea Apuzzo of Andrea's Restaurant in Metairie, Louisiana.
Melt the white chocolate in a double boiler over low heat. Stir until completely melted, then remove from heat.
Whip cream and set aside.
Slowly pour brandy and hot water into a bowl with egg yolks, whisking briskly until fully mixed.
Whisk in the melted white chocolate. Keep whisking to cool the mixture; this helps prevent the cream from breaking.
Beat egg whites and sugar until stiff but not dry; add vanilla as you beat.
Gently fold the meringue into the chocolate mixture with a wooden spoon.
Carefully fold in the whipped cream, ensuring uniform incorporation without incorporating air.
This recipe remains faithful to the original while improving clarity and consistency.
